items.title: Reuters Poll Says 30-Year Fixed Mortgage to Remain Above 6% Through 2028
items.author: Colin Robertson

items.title: Mortgage Rates Hold Steady Despite Worst Inflation Since 2023
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: It feels like we’re repeating history, and not the pleasant kind. Inflation readings came in at their worst since 2023, which incidentally was when the 30-year fixed seemed to peak this cycle around 8%. But this time around, mortgage rates are holding steady, seemingly undeterred by the rapid price increases. As for why, it might… Read More »

items.title: Total Interest Exceeds Your Home Price Once Mortgage Rates Are Above 6.4%
items.author: Colin Robertson

items.title: Did Everyone Forget Mortgage Rates Were 2-3% Back in 2012 Too?
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: I was on LinkedIn the other day and came across a post featuring a mortgage rate history chart with rates for each year. It started with 1975, when the 30-year fixed apparently averaged 9.0%, 1980 when it was supposedly 14.4%, and 2015 when it was 4.0%. For whatever reason they skipped a lot of years… Read More »

items.title: Mortgage Rates Now Face a Triple Threat
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: The conflict in Iran was arguably bad enough for mortgage rates. It sent them from the best levels since mid-2022 right back toward the high 6s again. To make matters worse, we’ve gotten a series of hot jobs reports too, including today’s BLS report beat. But that’s not all; a third threat is convexity hedging,… Read More »

items.title: Can You Have Two Mortgages at the Same Time?
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: It’s time for some more mortgage Q&A: “Can you have two mortgages at the same time?” This question came up recently and despite all the content on my site, it’s something I’ve never explicitly addressed. So let’s talk about it! In short, yes, you can two mortgages at the same time, or even three depending… Read More »

items.title: Mortgage Rates Now Facing War and Hot Jobs Headwinds
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: I keep warning folks that mortgage rates are going higher, possibly back to the 7% range. And the main reason is because the war in the Middle East is dragging out longer than anticipated. That could lead to even higher oil prices, which already spiked gas prices and are now affecting input costs on virtually… Read More »

items.title: A Top-10 Mortgage Lender Has Launched an Athleisure Clothing Line
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: This might be one of the strangest things I’ve ever heard come out of the mortgage industry. And the mortgage industry is no stranger to strange things. Chicago-based Rate (formerly known as Guaranteed Rate) has launched RateFit, a so-called “lifestyle brand.” They were the 7th largest mortgage lender in the country last year, but apparently… Read More »

items.title: Mortgage Rates Back on the Move Higher as U.S.-Iran Talks Stall
items.author: Colin Robertson
items.content: After what was a decent week for mortgage rates, in which they fell back closer to 6.50%, they appear to be on the rise again. The latest driver (surprise, surprise) is tensions in the Middle East and higher oil prices as a result. That pushed 10-bond yields back up about five basis points today, which… Read More »
